summary: Matched a large set of fighter ft_0881 routines, primarily fighter SFX channel start/stop/cleanup helpers and stale-move damage scaling. The PR converted many placeholder declarations into implemented C, refined SFX function prototypes to use u8 volume/pan parameters, and typed Fighter_804D6548 as float* to support staling-value indexing. The decomp bot reported 18 new matches and +4112 matched bytes for main/melee/ft/ft_0881.

key_files.reason: Main matching work: implemented previously stubbed ft_0881 routines for fighter SFX playback, channel cleanup, SFX array playback, stale-move scaling, and attack-id handling.
Updated prototypes with named parameters and u8 sfx_vol/sfx_pan types; replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for ft_80088998 with a typed void Fighter* signature.
Changed Fighter_804D6548 from UNK_T to float*, matching its use as an array of stale-move reduction values.
Header declaration updated for Fighter_804D6548 as float*.
Documentation extern updated to keep Fighter_804D6548 typing consistent.

what_changed: Implemented ft_80088510, ft_800885A8, ft_80088640, ftCo_800886D8, ft_800887CC, ft_80088828, ft_80088884, ft_800888E0, ft_8008893C, ft_80088998, ft_80088A50, ft_80088C5C, ft_80089118, and ft_80089228, replacing multiple placeholder comments.
Completed existing partial matches for ft_800881D8, ft_80088328, ft_80088478, and ft_800889F4 according to the bot report.

Reworked fighter SFX helpers around direct sentinel SFX IDs 0x83D60 and 0x83D61, lbAudioAx_80023870 calls, and per-player/per-costume audio channel expressions based on player_id * 2 plus offsets such as 0x1E, 0x2A, 0x36, 0x42, 0x4E, 0x5A, and 0x72.
Added SFX stop/reset helpers that play 0x83D61 with volume 0 and pan 0x40, then reset Fighter audio handle fields x2144, x2148, x214C, x2150, x2154, x2158, and x215C to -1.

Added broad fighter audio cleanup in ft_80088A50 and ft_80088C5C, including lbAudioAx_8002500C for x2164, lbAudioAx_80025038 for x2168, and lbAudioAx_80026510/lbAudioAx_800265C4 handling for a Fighter_GObj.
Added static ft_80089118 to compute stale-move scaling by walking StaleMoveTable entries backward from current_index and subtracting Fighter_804D6548[i] for matching move IDs.
Added ft_80089228 to bypass stale damage scaling at g_debugLevel >= 3, otherwise multiply the input damage/value by the factor returned from ft_80089118.
Updated ft_0881 includes to use explicit project include paths for sysdolphin/baselib/random.h, melee/db/db.h, fighter, ft_0877, inlines, lbaudio_ax, player, and plstale.
Typed Fighter_804D6548 as float* in source, header, and dox declarations.
smart_moves: Changing SFX volume and pan parameters from int to u8 removed repeated casts at call sites and made the prototypes match lbAudioAx_80023870 usage more naturally.
Typing Fighter_804D6548 as float* was necessary for clear and matchable stale-move damage math, where ft_80089118 indexes it as an array of floating-point penalties.

The implementation uses direct comparisons against full SFX IDs 0x83D60 and 0x83D61 instead of subtracting 0x80000 into a temporary, which simplified control flow and helped existing partial SFX functions reach 100%.
A small inline wrapper for the ftCo_800886D8 channel expression preserved the matched call shape while avoiding overcomplicating the public routine.
Using #pragma dont_inline around ft_80089118 documents and enforces an important codegen constraint for the stale-move helper.

The cleanup functions intentionally spell out repeated lbAudioAx_80023870 calls for paired channel indices, preserving the target instruction shape rather than abstracting into a loop that likely would not match.
decomp_lessons: For SFX helpers, exact integer widths on parameters can matter: u8 sfx_vol and u8 sfx_pan avoid extra casts and can alter matching codegen.
Global data typing should be driven by observed use. Fighter_804D6548 was not just an opaque pointer; stale-move code indexes it as float reduction data.
Repeated audio channel cleanup may need to remain unrolled to match retail code, even when a loop or helper would be more idiomatic C.
Sentinel audio IDs such as 0x83D60 and 0x83D61 can be compared directly when that produces the desired branch structure; introducing derived temporaries may hurt matching.
Stale-move table traversal wraps from index 0 back to 9 and stops early on move_id 0; preserving this exact traversal pattern is important for both behavior and matchability.
Debug-level gates can be part of gameplay math matching: ft_80089228 returns the original value unchanged when g_debugLevel >= 3.
naming_conventions: SFX function parameters were named sfx_id, sfx_vol, and sfx_pan in both source and header.
Fighter pointer parameters use fp; fighter object parameters use gobj; stale-move table variables use StaleMoveTable* and table/current_index/StaleMoves terminology.
Existing address-based function names were retained, including ft_800881D8, ft_80088A50, ft_80088C5C, ft_80089118, and ft_80089228.
The common-fighter-prefixed symbol ftCo_800886D8 was preserved for the 0886D8 routine.
Fighter audio handle fields remain offset-style names such as x2144, x2148, x214C, x2150, x2154, x2158, x215C, x2160, x2164, and x2168.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop for static f32 ft_80089118.
Kept an inline helper for the 0x72 audio-channel lbAudioAx_80023870 call used by ftCo_800886D8.
Removed initialization from a dummy stack array in ft_800892A0, changing u8 _[8] = { 0 } to u8 _[8], likely to match stack/codegen behavior.
Avoided unnecessary casts by matching function parameter types to lbAudioAx_80023870 expectations.
Unrolled sequential audio stop calls rather than factoring them into loops.
Used direct equality against fp->x2068_attackID without casting move_id to u32 in ft_800890D0 and inlineC0.

review_feedback.text: Reported GALE01 matched code increased to 44.18% (+0.11%, +4112 bytes) with 18 new matches in main/melee/ft/ft_0881.
No human review comments or review feedback were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type Fighter audio handle fields x2144 through x2168.
Search for additional uses of Fighter_804D6548 to confirm the stale-move reduction table layout and length.
Look for PRs that further document SFX sentinel IDs 0x83D60 and 0x83D61.
Search for later cleanup or deduplication of ft_80088A50 and ft_80088C5C audio reset sequences.
Find PRs touching Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr or StaleMoveTable to improve stale-move type names.
